paper_title: Automated Design Space Exploration for Optimized Deployment of DNN on Arm Cortex-A CPUs
main_focus: Optimizing the deployment of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) on resource-constrained edge devices, specifically Arm Cortex-A CPUs

problem_statement.challenge: DNNs are increasingly being deployed on edge devices with limited computing power, memory, and energy consumption
problem_statement.gap: There's a lack of research on cross-level optimization and design space exploration for end-to-end solutions
problem_statement.complexity: The space of approaches for DNN deployment is too large to fully explore manually

proposed_solution.framework: LPDNN, a deep learning framework for deployment and inference of DNN implementations
proposed_solution.method: QS-DNN, an automatic exploration framework based on Reinforcement Learning (RL)
proposed_solution.goal: Find optimized combinations of design options to speed up DNN inference and reduce memory for target platforms

contributions: Analysis of methods to improve DNN deployment across different software optimization levels
Introduction of techniques provided by LPDNN for DNN inference optimization
Presentation of QS-DNN, an RL-based automatic exploration framework
Results for state-of-the-art DNNs on various Arm Cortex-A CPU platforms
Comparison between RL and other search algorithms

results.scope: State-of-the-art DNNs on Arm Cortex-A CPU platforms

approaches: Efficient DNN architectures (MobileNets, SqueezeNet, hardware-aware NAS)

title: Automated Design Space Exploration for Optimized Deployment of DNN on Arm Cortex-A CPUs
